title: boechera elkoensis
intro: Boechera elkoensis, also known as Elko rockcress, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory agent and to have antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce the risk of infection. The health benefits of Boechera elkoensis include its potential to support immune function and to have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects on human health.
